title: Polyglot live-test sandbox — executing a generated framework wrapper live in the block workbench (isolation + runtime source)
summary: Prep research for decision #955 (blocks #912; Block Explorer epic #746, locus frontierui): how to EXECUTE (not just show) the React/Vue wrapper genWrapper emits and render it live with error surfacing. Tracing the real output (fui:tools/gen-wrapper/genWrapper.mjs:119) the React wrapper is literally `import React … return React.createElement('<fui-tag>', {ref,…}, children)` — so a live preview needs three things absent from the workbench today: (1) in-browser TS+JSX transpile, (2) a React/Vue runtime reachable from the preview, (3) the real custom element DEFINED where the wrapper runs. The workbench registers blocks via bundler-resolved dynamic imports (fui:workbench/registry.ts:149 `import('../blocks/droplist/AutoComplete')`) exposing NO stable per-block module URL to re-import elsewhere. The transpiler (esbuild-wasm vs Babel-standalone) is a confirmed impl detail; the two real axes are element isolation + framework-runtime source. PRIOR ART: Lit Playground (google/playground-elements) is THE closest analog — also web-components-first, also live-executes — and chose iframe `srcdoc` isolation (own execution context; console captured by monkey-patching console.* + postMessage to parent) + rewriting bare imports to CDN URLs (`import 'lit'`→unpkg/esm.sh). CodeSandbox Sandpack = iframe bundler (heavier); StackBlitz WebContainers = full Node-in-browser (maximalist); esm.sh/`esm.sh/tsx` = the no-build runtime-source + edge TSX-compile mechanism Lit-style tools point at; esbuild-wasm = `esbuild.transform()` in a Web Worker (initialize()+WASM url) and the only single-call transform that ALSO does module resolution/bundling (~10x slower than native, fine here); @babel/standalone lacks module resolution (weaker for a wrapper that imports react). LOAD-BEARING PLATFORM FACTS grounding the defaults: import maps are document-scoped and resolve-once (a fresh `<iframe srcdoc>` gets a fresh import map per preview; same-doc cannot re-resolve frameworks across previews/targets); customElements.define throws on re-definition (same-doc can only reuse the workbench's already-registered element, an iframe gets a clean registry); executed wrapper code is framework code not host DOM (a same-doc throw can break the workbench shell; an iframe contains it). HOW IT RESHAPES THE FORKS: Fork A (element isolation) — Lit Playground + bias-toward-separation move the best-end-state default from the item's interim A2 (same-document) to A1 (iframe srcdoc + own import-map); the genuine counterweight keeping A2 live is that A1 must get the element DEFINED inside the frame, needing a stable per-block module URL the registry doesn't expose (real build cost in #912) — but that is build effort (prioritization, filed to the build per fork-is-not-a-prioritization-tool), not a merit defect, so the fork rules A1 on merit. Fork B (framework-runtime source) — invariant: the runtime must NEVER enter FUI's shipped bundle (B-bundle-into-FUI is the flawed excluded branch, a web-components lib shipping framework runtimes). Among framework-free options the item defaulted to CDN import-map (esm.sh), but the item ALSO requires an e2e plan not dependent on external network ('vendor the runtimes or stub'); if vendoring must exist for CI anyway, CDN means maintaining TWO paths (CDN dev + vendored CI), so the merit default moves to a single LOCALLY-VENDORED devDependency served into the sandbox iframe — offline + deterministic, framework-free (a devDep is not the shipped bundle), and (paired with the already-local esbuild-wasm) one path for dev + CI. The two forks are COUPLED: B's framework-free isolation is cleanest when the runtime loads INSIDE the iframe (A1), not the workbench document (A2); A1 + local-vendored mirrors Lit Playground while honoring framework-free + offline-CI. CLASSIFICATION: FUI-owned devtool inside the workbench (fui:workbench/, locus frontierui) — not a WE standard (impl-is-not-a-standard, #809 workbench is FUI-owned); no new protocol (consumes only the ratified CEM #626 the wrapper is generated from, + #506 verdict/#891 runner for the downstream badge #913); no runtime-DI seam (author/dev-time execution, not a registry the running standard consults); transpiler stays swappable impl. RECOMMENDATION: Fork A→A1 iframe srcdoc (~70%; residual: needs a per-block module URL + the #815 one-document-no-channel philosophy is A2's principled counter — flag for skeptic pass); Fork B→local-vendored devDep served to the sandbox iframe (~75%; residual: esm.sh CDN is less wiring and is what Lit Playground ships); supported-by-default: esbuild-wasm in a Web Worker, console/error via the Lit-Playground postMessage monkey-patch, e2e reuses the vendored runtime (no external network — resolves the item's e2e ask). Full grounding in reports/2026-06-18-polyglot-live-test-sandbox.md.
